Here’s a 7-day, gluten-free, lactose-free, shellfish-free meal plan that steers clear of onions, keeps salt and sugar moderate, leans on meat, potatoes and your favourite cuisines, and uses simple, budget-friendly ingredients.  Where you’d normally use onion you can omit it or—if you tolerate it—add a pinch of asafoetida (hing) for Indian dishes.  Garlic is included where helpful.  Prep once, eat twice when you can; swap lunches and dinners around if you like.

Day 1  
Breakfast  
 • GF rolled oats cooked in almond milk, stirred in 1 tsp chia seeds and ½ sliced banana  
Lunch  
 • Grilled chicken breast (120 g), sliced over mixed lettuce, cucumber, tomato; dress with 1 Tbsp olive oil + lemon juice  
Dinner  
 • Beef & potato curry  
   – Sauté 200 g lean beef mince with 2 cloves garlic, 1" grated ginger, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp chili flakes  
   – Add 2 diced potatoes, 1 tomato (chopped), 200 mL water; simmer 20 min.  
   – Serve over boiled rice (⅔ cup cooked)  
Snack  
 • Carrot sticks + hummus (chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, salt to taste)

Day 2  
Breakfast  
 • 2 scrambled eggs with a handful baby spinach  
Lunch  
 • Mexican chicken bowl  
   – Leftover grilled chicken (120 g), ½ cup cooked rice, ¼ cup corn kernels, diced capsicum, fresh salsa (tomato, coriander, lime)  
Dinner  
 • Thai-style fried rice  
   – Sauté 1 beaten egg, remove. In same pan, 120 g diced chicken, 1 cup mixed veg (carrot, capsicum, beans), 1 ½ cups cooked rice, 1 Tbsp gluten-free soy sauce, a handful Thai basil (if available). Return egg.  
Snack  
 • Apple slices + 1 Tbsp peanut butter

Day 3  
Breakfast  
 • Green smoothie: 1 banana, handful spinach, 1 Tbsp peanut butter, 200 mL almond milk  
Lunch  
 • Beef tacos  
   – Brown 150 g beef mince with ½ tsp each cumin & paprika, pinch chili  
   – Serve in 2 corn tortillas with shredded lettuce, tomato, dairy-free cheese  
Dinner  
 • Potato & chickpea “dal”  
   – Sauté 1 tsp mustard seeds in oil, add 2 cloves garlic, 1" ginger, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder  
   – Stir in 1 cup cooked chickpeas, 2 diced potatoes, 200 mL water; simmer until tender  
   – Serve with rice or gluten-free flatbread  
Snack  
 • 2 rice cakes topped with mashed avocado

Day 4  
Breakfast  
 • 2 slices gluten-free bread, mashed avocado, 1 poached egg  
Lunch  
 • Chicken & veg stir-fry  
   – 120 g chicken strips, sliced carrot & bok choy, 1 Tbsp GF soy sauce, 1 tsp grated ginger  
   – Serve over ½ cup cooked rice  
Dinner  
 • Mexican stuffed capsicums  
   – Halve 2 capsicums, fill with 150 g beef mince cooked with minced garlic, cumin, tomato paste, a splash water  
   – Top with dairy-free cheese, bake 15 min at 180 °C  
Snack  
 • Cucumber slices + guacamole (mashed avocado, lime, tomato, salt)

Day 5  
Breakfast  
 • Buckwheat pancakes (buckwheat flour, egg, almond milk) topped with mixed berries  
Lunch  
 • Tuna lettuce wraps  
   – 1 can tuna in spring water, drained, mixed with 1 Tbsp mayo, diced celery, pepper; wrap in lettuce leaves  
Dinner  
 • Thai peanut chicken  
   – Marinate 120 g chicken in 1 Tbsp peanut butter, 1 Tbsp GF soy sauce, garlic; stir-fry, serve over rice noodles  
Snack  
 • Handful mixed nuts

Day 6  
Breakfast  
 • Egg muffins (whisk 3 eggs + handful spinach + diced capsicum; pour into muffin tin; bake 15 min at 180 °C)  
Lunch  
 • Leftover beef & potato curry over rice  
Dinner  
 • Grilled steak (150 g)  
   – Season sparingly with salt & pepper  
   – Roast 2 chopped potatoes tossed in olive oil (200 °C, 25 min)  
   – Steam broccoli on side  
Snack  
 • A small bunch grapes

Day 7  
Breakfast  
 • Chia pudding (3 Tbsp chia, 200 mL almond milk, vanilla if you like) topped with berries  
Lunch  
 • Indian spiced red lentil soup  
   – Sauté 1 tsp mustard seeds, garlic, ginger; add 1 cup red lentils, 1 tsp turmeric, 4 cups water; simmer until thick.  
   – Season with salt & lemon; serve with rice or GF flatbread  
Dinner  
 • Chicken tikka salad  
   – Marinate 120 g chicken in dairy-free yogurt (e.g. coconut), 1 tsp garam masala, garlic, ginger; grill or bake  
   – Serve on greens with cucumber, tomato, lemon  
Snack  
 • Air-popped popcorn (light salt)

Weekly Staples Shopping List (approx.)  
 • Proteins: 1 kg chicken breast, 500 g lean beef mince, 2 steaks (or extra mince), dozen eggs, 1 can tuna, 1 can chickpeas, 1 cup red lentils  
 • Carbs: 1 kg rice, potatoes (1–2 kg), GF oats, buckwheat flour, GF bread or corn tortillas, rice noodles  
 • Veg & fruit: spinach, lettuce, tomato, cucumber, capsicum, carrot, broccoli, bok choy (or other greens), banana, mixed berries, apple, grapes  
 • Pantry: almond milk, chia seeds, peanut butter, mixed nuts, canned tomatoes, GF soy sauce, tahini, mayo, spices (turmeric, cumin, coriander, paprika, chili flakes, garam masala), garlic, ginger, olive oil  
 • Dairy-free cheese & yogurt

- lower in salt
- avoids bitter veg-heavy meals

Good flavor boosters for you:
- lime juice in small amounts
- coconut milk
- paprika
- cumin
- ginger
- garlic
- fresh herbs if affordable

Avoid overdoing:
- too much chili
- too much tamari/soy sauce
- very bitter greens
- heavily charred foods

Of course. Here is a cheap, simple, and flavourful 7-day meal plan designed specifically for your needs, preferences, and super-taster palate.

### Guiding Principles for Your Plan:
*   **Gluten-Free:** All meals use naturally gluten-free grains like rice or corn.
*   **Lactose-Free:** No dairy. We'll use plant-based alternatives or omit it entirely.
*   **Allium-Free:** **No onion, garlic, leeks, or shallots.** This is the biggest challenge, but we'll use creative substitutes like celery, fennel, and asafoetida (a common Indian substitute for onion/garlic flavour).
*   **Flavour Focus:** For a super-taster, we'll build deep, complex flavours without overloading on salt or sugar. We'll use acids (lemon, lime, vinegar), fresh herbs, and spices.
*   **Budget-Friendly:** Uses affordable cuts of meat, seasonal vegetables, and bulk staples.

**Key Flavour Substitutes:**
*   **Instead of Onion/Garlic:** Use the white part of **spring onions (scallions)** or **chives**. Your intolerance is likely to alliums, and the green parts are milder and often tolerated. *Please check with yourself if these are okay.* If not, use a pinch of **asafoetida (hing)** powder added to oil at the start of cooking – it provides a savoury, umami base. Celery or fennel also add a good aromatic base.
*   **Instead of Dairy:** Coconut milk/cream, lactose-free yogurts (check labels), and nutritional yeast for a "cheesy" flavour.

---

### The 7-Day Meal Plan

#### **Day 1: Mexican Fiesta**
*   **Lunch:** **Large Chicken & Rice Bowl.** Leftover shredded chicken from dinner on a bed of rice with black beans (canned, rinsed), corn, diced tomato, avocado, and coriander. A squeeze of lime.
*   **Dinner:** **Slow-Cooker Mexican Pulled Chicken.** Place chicken thighs, a tin of diced tomatoes, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp oregano, and a pinch of chilli powder in a slow cooker. Cook on low for 6-8 hours. Shred and serve with **corn tortillas**, quick-pickled red cabbage (shredded cabbage in apple cider vinegar), and avocado.

#### **Day 2: Thai Inspired**
*   **Lunch:** **Leftover Pulled Chicken Salad.** Use the leftover chicken in a salad with lettuce, cucumber, shredded carrot, and a Thai-inspired dressing (see below).
*   **Dinner:** **Simple Thai Pork Mince (Larb).** Brown 500g pork mince. Add 2 tbsp fish sauce, juice of 2 limes, and 1-2 tsp chilli flakes. Off the heat, stir through chopped mint, coriander, and **finely sliced spring onion greens**. Serve with crisp lettuce cups (iceberg or cos) to scoop and eat.

#### **Day 3: Hearty & Homely**
*   **Lunch:** **Leftover Larb** wrapped in lettuce cups.
*   **Dinner:** **Classic Shepherd's Pie (Gluten & Dairy Free).** Brown 500g beef mince with diced carrots and celery. Add 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 cup beef stock, and Worcestershire sauce (check for gluten!). Top with a huge layer of creamy mashed potatoes (made with olive oil or lactose-free margarine instead of butter/milk) and bake until golden.

#### **Day 4: Curry Night**
*   **Lunch:** **Leftover Shepherd's Pie.**
*   **Dinner:** **Simple Red Beef Curry.** Use a gluten-free Thai red curry paste (***check label for garlic/onion*** – some brands are clean). Fry 1 tbsp paste in a dash of coconut milk, add strips of beef rump steak and vegetables like green beans and bamboo shoots. Simmer in coconut milk until cooked. Serve with jasmine rice.

#### **Day 5: Fresh & Zesty**
*   **Lunch:** **Deconstructed Curry Bowl.** Leftover curry and rice served cold as a salad with extra cucumber and a squeeze of lime.
*   **Dinner:** **Pan-Fried Fish with Lemon Herb Potatoes.** Season fish fillets (e.g., snapper, barramundi) with salt, pepper, and dill. Pan-fry until crispy. Serve with boiled potatoes tossed in olive oil, lemon zest, parsley, and dill.

#### **Day 6: Indian Feast**
*   **Lunch:** **Leftover fish and potatoes.**
*   **Dinner:** **Chicken & Potato Curry.** Sauté chicken thighs and diced potatoes. Add 1 tbsp grated ginger, 1 tbsp ground coriander, 2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, and a **pinch of asafoetida (hing)**. Add a tin of diced tomatoes and simmer until chicken is cooked and potatoes are tender. Stir through coconut cream at the end. Serve with basmati rice.

#### **Day 7: Prep Day / Simple Dinner**
*   **Lunch:** **Leftover Indian Curry.**
*   **Dinner:** **Hearty Beef & Vegetable Soup.** Brown stewing beef chunks. Add diced carrots, celery, potatoes, swede, and enough beef stock to cover. Add a bay leaf and thyme. Simmer for 1.5-2 hours until beef is tender. Season with black pepper and a little salt at the end. (Makes leftovers for Monday lunch!).

---

### Staples to Buy:
*   **Proteins:** Chicken thighs, pork mince, beef mince, beef rump or stewing steak, white fish fillets.
*   **Vegetables:** Potatoes, carrots, celery, tomatoes, lettuce, cucumber, avocado, limes/lemons, coriander, mint, spring onions (for greens only).
*   **Pantry:** Rice (jasmine & basmati), corn tortillas, tinned tomatoes, tinned black beans, coconut milk/cream, gluten-free beef stock, fish sauce, spices (cumin, paprika, smoked paprika, oregano, chilli powder/flakes, coriander, turmeric, asafoetida/hing), Worcestershire sauce (GF).

### Sample Dressings & Sauces:
*   **Thai Dressing:** 3 parts fish sauce, 2 parts lime juice, 1 part water, a pinch of chilli flakes, and a tiny bit of coconut sugar or maple syrup (omit if too sweet).
*   **Simple Vinaigrette:** 3 parts extra virgin olive oil, 1 part red wine vinegar or lemon juice, mustard powder, salt, and pepper.

Enjoy your week of delicious, tailored eating

Below is a 7‐day meal plan that works with your dietary needs, budget concerns, and flavor preferences. This plan emphasizes simple recipes that use inexpensive, versatile ingredients (like potatoes, rice, and affordable cuts of meat) and incorporates Mexican, Thai, and Indian flavor influences. All recipes avoid shellfish, gluten (use gluten‐free grains and corn tortillas where needed), lactose (use lactose‐free dairy or substitutes), and onions. You can adjust spice levels since you have a super‐tasting gene—if something tastes too potent, reduce the chili or aromatic spices, and remember that “less is more” when you’re extra sensitive. Feel free to batch‐cook items like rice or potatoes for extra savings and ease.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 1

• Breakfast: Potato & Spinach Scramble
  – Ingredients: Eggs, small diced potato (pre‐boiled or sautéed until tender), handful spinach, garlic (or garlic-infused oil for a milder taste), salt/pepper.
  – Directions: Sauté the potato cubes in a little garlic-infused oil, add spinach until wilted, then pour beaten eggs over and scramble until just set.

• Lunch: Chicken Tortilla Soup (Onion-free)
  – Ingredients: Chicken breast (or thigh), canned diced tomatoes, garlic, chopped bell peppers, corn kernels, chili powder, cumin, gluten-free (corn) tortilla chips.
  – Directions: Bring water or low-sodium chicken broth to a simmer with garlic, bell peppers, tomatoes, and spices. Add diced chicken until cooked through. Top a serving bowl with crumbled corn tortilla chips for crunch.

• Dinner: Thai Basil Chicken
  – Ingredients: Minced chicken, garlic, fresh Thai basil leaves, chopped red bell peppers and green beans, a dash of fish sauce (if tolerated, or replace with gluten-free soy sauce for umami), lime juice, and steamed rice.
  – Directions: In a hot pan, sauté garlic briefly, add chicken and cook until no longer pink. Toss in the vegetables and basil leaves; finish with a squeeze of lime and serve over rice.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 2

• Breakfast: Gluten-Free Oats
  – Ingredients: Gluten-free rolled oats, lactose-free milk (or water), a sliced banana, and a sprinkle of nuts (optional).
  – Directions: Cook oats per package directions, stir in milk or water, and top with banana slices and nuts.

• Lunch: Indian-Spiced Ground Beef & Potato Stew
  – Ingredients: Lean ground beef, diced potatoes, canned tomatoes, garlic, ginger, spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, a pinch of chili powder), water or broth.
  – Directions: Brown the beef with garlic and ginger, add diced potatoes and tomatoes, then stir in spices. Simmer until potatoes are tender. Adjust spice amounts gradually given your sensitivity.

• Dinner: Mexican Chicken Bowl
  – Ingredients: Chicken breast strips, lime juice, a mix of chili powder and cumin, cooked rice, black beans, diced tomato, diced avocado, fresh cilantro.
  – Directions: Marinate the chicken briefly in lime juice and spices, then grill or pan-sauté. Assemble bowls with rice, beans, chicken, and toppings. You can also add a drizzle of lactose-free sour cream if desired.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 3

• Breakfast: Vegetable Omelette
  – Ingredients: Eggs, fresh spinach, diced mushrooms, garlic (or garlic-infused oil), salt/pepper.
  – Directions: Sauté spinach and mushrooms briefly, then add whisked eggs and cook until set.

• Lunch: Thai Beef Stir-Fry
  – Ingredients: Thinly sliced beef (look for economical cuts), garlic, chopped red and yellow bell peppers, thin slices of carrots, Thai basil leaves, a splash of gluten-free soy sauce, and steamed rice or rice noodles.
  – Directions: Quickly stir-fry garlic, beef, and vegetables on high heat; finish with basil and soy sauce. Serve immediately.

• Dinner: Indian-Inspired Tandoori Chicken Legs (Legs or Thighs are often budget-friendly)
  – Ingredients: Chicken pieces, lactose-free yogurt or coconut yogurt, garlic, ginger, lemon juice, tandoori spice mix (ensure no onion powder), and a side of roasted potato wedges.
  – Directions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, spices, garlic, ginger, and lemon juice for at least an hour. Roast in the oven; serve with oven-roasted potatoes tossed in cumin and coriander.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 4

• Breakfast: Fried Eggs & Potato Hash
  – Ingredients: Eggs, diced boiled potatoes, bell peppers, garlic, a pinch of salt/pepper.
  – Directions: Sauté the potatoes and peppers with garlic until they crisp up, then top with fried eggs.

• Lunch: Mexican Ground Turkey Lettuce Wraps
  – Ingredients: Ground turkey, garlic, chili powder, cumin, diced tomato, avocado, and crisp lettuce leaves.
  – Directions: Brown the turkey with garlic and spices, then spoon into washed lettuce leaves. Top with diced tomato and avocado.

• Dinner: Thai Red Curry with Chicken & Potatoes
  – Ingredients: Chicken chunks, diced potatoes, red bell peppers, garlic, ginger, a homemade or trusted Thai red curry paste (check for no onion ingredients), coconut milk (lactose-free), and rice.
  – Directions: Simmer garlic and ginger in a little oil, add curry paste, then chicken and potatoes. Pour coconut milk, add peppers last, and simmer until chicken and potatoes are tender. Serve with rice.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 5

• Breakfast: Fruit Smoothie
  – Ingredients: Lactose-free milk or a dairy-free alternative (almond or coconut milk if preferred), banana, a handful of spinach, and a few ice cubes.
  – Directions: Blend until smooth.

• Lunch: Indian-Spiced Chicken & Potato Soup
  – Ingredients: Diced chicken, small potato cubes, canned tomatoes, garlic, ginger, cumin, and coriander, with water or a light chicken broth.
  – Directions: Simmer all ingredients until potatoes are tender and the chicken is cooked through. Adjust spice levels slowly.

• Dinner: Mexican Steak Fajitas (Onion-Free)
  – Ingredients: Lean steak strips, sliced bell peppers, garlic, fajita seasoning (ensure a blend without onion), gluten-free (corn) tortillas, and a side salad with lettuce and diced tomato.
  – Directions: Sear steak and peppers with garlic and seasoning on high heat. Serve with warmed tortillas and salad.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 6

• Breakfast: Scrambled Eggs with Potatoes & Peppers
  – Ingredients: Eggs, pre-cooked diced potatoes, sliced bell peppers, garlic, pepper.
  – Directions: Sauté the peppers and potatoes briefly, add whisked eggs, and cook until softly scrambled.

• Lunch: Thai Peanut Chicken Salad
  – Ingredients: Grilled chicken pieces, mixed greens (or shredded cabbage), grated carrots, cucumber slices, and a dressing of peanut butter, lime juice, a little water to thin, garlic, and finely chopped red chili (adjust to taste).
  – Directions: Toss salad ingredients with the dressing and top with sliced grilled chicken.

• Dinner: Indian-Style Beef Curry with Potatoes
  – Ingredients: Beef stew meat, diced potatoes, canned tomatoes, garlic, ginger, curry powder and garam masala, water/broth.
  – Directions: Brown beef with garlic and ginger, add potatoes, tomatoes, spices, and simmer slowly until meat is tender. Serve with rice.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 7

• Breakfast: Warm Gluten-Free Porridge
  – Ingredients: Gluten-free oats or quinoa flakes, water or lactose-free milk, topped with seasonal fruit (such as apples or berries).
  – Directions: Cook as per package directions and top with fruit.

• Lunch: Mexican Quinoa (or Rice) Bowl
  – Ingredients: Cooked quinoa or rice, black beans, grilled chicken or beef strips, chopped tomato, diced avocado, chopped fresh cilantro, lime juice, and a mild chili powder.
  – Directions: Layer the bowl with grains, bean, meat, and fresh toppings. Drizzle with lime juice.

• Dinner: Thai Green Curry with Chicken & Potatoes
  – Ingredients: Chicken chunks, diced potatoes, green beans (or snap peas), garlic, Thai green curry paste (check label for onion-free), coconut milk, and rice.
  – Directions: Sauté garlic then add green curry paste, followed by chicken, potatoes, and green beans. Pour in coconut milk and simmer until everything is tender. Serve over rice.

──────────────────────────────
Additional Tips for Budget & Flavor:
1. Buy in bulk when possible (rice, potatoes, oats) and look for sales on meats.
2. Make extra portions of curries, soups, or stews to serve as leftovers for lunch the next day.
3. Always taste as you cook—since you’re sensitive to strong flavors, start with smaller amounts of spicy ingredients and adjust gradually.
4. Use garlic-infused oils if you’d like a hint of garlic without the intensity of whole garlic if needed.
5. Ensure all packaged items (like curry pastes or tortillas) are checked for your allergens (gluten/lactose/onion).

This meal plan provides a balanced mix of protein, carbohydrates, and vegetables while invoking the flavors you enjoy. Enjoy your week of cooking and dining!

Breakfast

 • Adjust portion sizes or add a small lactose-free protein shake if you need more calories.

Each day = breakfast, lunch, dinner. Adjust portions for your appetite.

 Breakfast  
  • Scrambled eggs (2–3) with chopped tomato & baby spinach (small handful)

 Lunch  
  • Grilled chicken salad: mixed lettuce, cucumber, tomato, shredded carrot, sliced grilled chicken thigh, olive oil + lemon dressing  
 Dinner  
  • Beef “taco bowl”: sauté lean ground beef with garlic, cumin, paprika; serve over lettuce, black beans (canned, rinsed), corn kernels, diced tomato, avocado, squeeze of lime  

Day 2  
 Breakfast  
  • Potato-pepper hash: diced potato + red bell pepper sautéed in olive oil & garlic, topped with a fried egg  
 Lunch  
  • Red lentil soup: red lentils + diced carrot, celery, garlic + ground cumin simmered in water or stock; serve with 1 slice gluten-free toast

 Breakfast

Tips

## How This Plan Meets Your Needs

| Requirement | How It’s Handled |
|-------------|------------------|
| **Gluten‑free** | All grains are rice, quinoa, gluten‑free oats, corn tortillas, rice noodles; no wheat‑based sauces. |
| **Lactose‑free** | Uses lactose‑free yogurts, coconut milk, almond milk, dairy‑free cheese alternatives. |
| **Shellfish‑free** | No shrimp, fish sauce is used only in tiny amounts (you can replace with a pinch of salt + extra lime if you’re uncomfortable). |
| **Onion‑free** | Recipes rely on garlic, ginger, scallion greens, chives, herbs, and spices for depth. |
| **Low‑salt & low‑sweet** | Salt is kept to ≤½ tsp per dish; sweeteners are optional and limited to ≤1 tsp honey or sugar. |
| **Super‑taster** (heightened bitter perception) | Avoids bitter greens (e.g., kale, arugula) and heavy bitter spices; balances flavors with citrus, fresh herbs, and a touch of natural sweetness (fruit, honey). |
| **Budget‑friendly** | Core proteins (ground beef/turkey, chicken thighs, eggs, canned beans) are cheap; starches are bulk rice/potatoes; herbs/spices bought in small jars last months. |
| **Simple prep** | Most meals are one‑pot or 2‑step, ≤30 min total cooking time.  Batch‑cook rice/quinoa once for 2‑3 days to save time. |
| **Cultural cravings** | Mexican (tacos, bowls, fajitas), Thai (coconut curry, basil pork, peanut noodles), Indian (dal, tikka, chickpea curry) are each represented twice. |

## Practical Tips for Your “Super‑Tasting” Gene & Flavor Preferences  

1. **Mild‑but‑bright flavors** – Use fresh herbs (cilantro, parsley, rosemary) and citrus zest/juice to add depth without overwhelming salt or sweetness.  
2. **Control salt** – Choose low‑sodium tamari, soy‑free fish sauce, and season with spices + a squeeze of lime instead of extra salt.  
3. **Avoid “very sweet”** – Keep honey or sweet sauces to ≤ ½ tsp per serving; let fruit provide natural sweetness.  
4. **Texture variety** – Combine crunchy (nuts, roasted potatoes) with soft (mashed sweet‑potato, creamy coconut‑milk) to keep meals interesting.  
5. **Batch‑cook staples** – Cook a big pot of rice, quinoa, and boiled potatoes on Sunday; store in the fridge for quick assembly.  
6. **One‑pan meals** – Most dinner recipes are 1‑pan or skillet dishes → less cleanup, less time.

## Super-Taster Notes

Since you carry the super-tasting gene, this plan is designed with the following in mind:

- **Bitterness is minimised** — limited use of raw cruciferous veg; broccoli is always cooked. No kale or rocket.
- **Umami-forward flavours** — fish sauce, tamari, slow-cooked meats, and tomato paste provide depth without relying on salt.
- **Balanced spice** — curry pastes and spice blends are used at moderate levels. You can always scale up, but starting moderate avoids overwhelming a sensitive palate.
- **Richness via coconut cream** — replaces dairy and adds smooth, rounded flavour that super-tasters tend to enjoy.
- **Acidity as a flavour tool** — lime juice and rice wine vinegar brighten dishes without needing excess salt or sugar.

---

## Meal Prep Tips

1. **Sunday arvo:** Cook a big batch of jasmine rice for the week. Refrigerate in portions.
2. **Cook pork shoulder on Sunday** for tacos — leftovers can be frozen.
3. **Boil 4–6 eggs** at the start of the week for quick snacks.
4. **Double the keema or butter chicken** — freezes brilliantly for lazy nights.
5. **Potatoes:** Bake 3–4 at once; reheat throughout the week for hash, sides, or loaded potatoes.

## Your Profile Summary
- **TDEE (estimated):** ~2,100–2,200 kcal/day (moderate activity assumed)
- **Goal:** Maintenance / balanced nutrition on a budget
- **Avoid:** Shellfish, gluten, lactose, onion, overly salty/sweet foods
- **Super-taster note:** Recipes emphasise balanced, layered flavours without bitterness extremes. Spice levels are moderate and aromatic rather than harsh. Vegetables are cooked to mellow bitterness.

### Budget Strategy
- Base meals around **rice, potatoes, eggs, canned beans/lentils, and cheaper cuts of meat** (chicken thighs, beef mince, pork shoulder)
- Buy spices in bulk (cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, turmeric, chilli flakes, garam masala, curry powder)
- Use coconut milk/cream (lactose-free) for richness
- All sauces/marinades are gluten-free (tamari instead of soy sauce, check curry pastes for GF)
- No onion — substitute with the **green part of spring onions (scallions)** and **asafoetida (hing)** for depth

Below is a 7‐day meal plan designed to be simple, budget–friendly, and suited to your dietary needs and taste preferences. This plan avoids shellfish, gluten, lactose, and onions while featuring hearty meats, potatoes, and flavors inspired by Mexican, Thai, and Indian cuisines. (Feel free to adjust portion sizes or flavors as needed since you have a super‐tasting gene and may prefer milder seasonings.) 

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 1

• Breakfast: Oatmeal Bowl
 – Gluten–free rolled oats cooked in lactose–free milk (e.g., almond or coconut) with sliced banana and a handful of berries.
 – A sprinkle of cinnamon for warmth.

• Lunch: Grilled Chicken Salad 
 – Mixed greens (spinach/lettuce), sliced cucumber, and diced bell pepper.
 – Grilled chicken breast (season lightly with garlic, lemon, and a pinch of salt).
 – A drizzle of olive oil and lemon juice as dressing.

• Dinner: Mexican–Style Beef & Potato Skillet  
 – Sauté lean ground beef with diced potatoes, chopped tomatoes, black beans, and a minced garlic clove.
 – Season with cumin, smoked paprika, and a pinch of chili powder (adjust for your sensitivity).
 – Serve warm; garnish with fresh cilantro.

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 2

• Breakfast: Scrambled Eggs & Toast  
 – Scrambled eggs, lightly seasoned with salt and pepper.
 – Serve with gluten–free toast (ensure no dairy butter if intolerant) and a side of sliced tomato.

• Lunch: Thai–Inspired Chicken Salad  
 – Poached or grilled chicken, shredded lettuce, grated carrot, sliced cucumber.
 – Toss with fresh basil and mint.
 – Dress with a fresh squeeze of lime, a dash of fish sauce substitute (or salt if needed), and a tiny bit of garlic.

• Dinner: Indian–Style Chicken Curry  
 – Use chicken thighs or breasts cut into pieces.
 – Sauté garlic and grated ginger in a bit of oil (skip onions).
 – Stir in diced potatoes and tomatoes with spices such as turmeric, cumin, and coriander.
 – Pour in a can of coconut milk (a lactose–free alternative) and simmer until potatoes are tender.
 – Serve with steamed rice.

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 3

• Breakfast: Banana Peanut Smoothie  
 – Blend a banana with a spoonful of peanut butter, a handful of spinach, almond milk, and a few ice cubes.

• Lunch: Beef Stir–Fry  
 – Slice lean beef thinly.
 – Stir–fry with mixed veggies (red and green bell peppers, zucchini, carrots) using garlic and grated ginger as the aromatic base.
 – Serve over jasmine or brown rice.

• Dinner: Mexican Chicken Fajitas  
 – Marinate chicken strips in lime juice, garlic, and a pinch each of cumin and chili powder.
 – Sauté with sliced bell peppers.
 – Serve in gluten–free tortillas with a homemade salsa (blend tomatoes, cilantro, and lime—avoid onions).

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 4

• Breakfast: Overnight Oats  
 – Mix gluten–free oats with almond milk, a diced apple, and a dash of cinnamon.
 – Refrigerate overnight and enjoy in the morning.

• Lunch: Thai Beef Salad  
 – Use thinly sliced, quickly seared beef over a bed of lettuce, cucumber ribbons, and shredded carrots.
 – Add fresh mint and basil.
 – Dress with a light mix of lime juice, a dash of garlic, and a tiny splash of olive oil.

• Dinner: Indian Lamb Stew  
 – Use lamb cubes (or beef if preferred) simmered with diced potatoes, carrots, tomatoes, garlic, ginger, and spices (such as garam masala, cumin, turmeric).
 – Slow–cook until flavors meld.
 – Serve with plain steamed rice.

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 5

• Breakfast: Eggs & Gluten–Free Toast  
 – Prepare eggs to your liking (fried or scrambled using olive oil instead of butter).
 – Serve with gluten–free toast and a side of sliced avocado (optional).

• Lunch: Chicken & Potato Soup  
 – Simmer diced chicken breast with potatoes, carrots, and celery (if tolerated) in a chicken broth (check that it’s free of gluten and dairy).
 – Season gently with garlic and herbs (thyme, bay leaf).

• Dinner: Mexican Pork Tacos  
 – Sauté lean ground pork with garlic, diced potatoes, and tomatoes.
 – Season with cumin and chili powder (keep it mild).
 – Serve in gluten–free tortillas with a homemade salsa (no onions) and a squeeze of lime.

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 6

• Breakfast: Rice Pudding  
 – Make a simple rice pudding by simmering rice in almond milk with a bit of cinnamon and raisins.
 – Enjoy warm or chilled.

• Lunch: Thai Peanut Chicken Wrap  
 – Use shredded grilled chicken, lettuce, and grated carrots.
 – Toss with a homemade Thai peanut sauce (blend a small amount of natural peanut butter, a dash of lime juice, garlic, water to thin, and a hint of soy sauce substitute that is gluten–free).
 – Wrap in gluten–free flatbread or lettuce leaves.

• Dinner: Indian Chicken Tikka with Roasted Potatoes  
 – Marinate chicken pieces in a mixture of coconut yogurt (or dairy–free yogurt), garlic, ginger, turmeric, and cumin.
 – Grill or bake until cooked through.
 – Roast potato wedges with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of mild spices.
 – Serve together with a side of steamed greens.

─────────────────────────────  
DAY 7

• Breakfast: Scrambled Eggs & Spinach  
 – Prepare scrambled eggs with a handful of spinach.
 – Serve with gluten–free toast.

• Lunch: Mexican Beef & Potato Chili  
 – Cook lean ground beef with diced potatoes, garlic, canned tomatoes, black beans, and spices (cumin, paprika, a touch of chili powder).
 – Simmer until thick and flavorful.
 – Garnish with freshly chopped cilantro.

• Dinner: Thai Red Curry with Chicken  
 – Sauté chicken pieces with garlic and ginger.
 – Add diced potatoes and red bell peppers.
 – Stir in a can of coconut milk, red curry paste (taste–adjusted for sensitivity), and a handful of Thai basil.
 – Serve with steamed rice.

─────────────────────────────  
Tips and Adjustments:

1. Seasoning: Since you’re super–tasting, start with smaller amounts of spices and salt, and adjust gradually. Fresh herbs, citrus juices, and milder spice blends can enhance flavor without overwhelming your palate.

2. Pantry Staples: Buying items like rice, gluten–free oats, canned tomatoes, beans, and spices in bulk can keep costs down. Use seasonal vegetables where possible.

3. Prep Ahead: Many of these dishes (like soups, stews, and marinades) can be prepped in advance or made in larger quantities for leftovers—saving time and money.

4. Ingredient Swaps: If any ingredient isn’t available, feel free to substitute with a suitable alternative that meets your dietary restrictions. For instance, if you want more variety in protein, swap chicken with turkey or another lean meat.

Here's a simple and affordable 7-day meal plan tailored to your requirements and preferences:

Day 1:
Breakfast: Gluten-free oatmeal with banana and cinnamon
Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with roasted potatoes and steamed broccoli

Breakfast: Smoothie bowl with almond milk, banana, spinach, and chia seeds
Lunch: Quinoa salad with grilled vegetables and chickpeas
Dinner: Pork chops with apple sauce, mashed potatoes, and steamed carrots

Day 5:
Breakfast: Gluten-free pancakes with maple syrup and fresh fruit
Lunch: Chicken and vegetable soup (no onion) with gluten-free crackers
Dinner: Mexican-style fajitas with beef, bell peppers, and corn tortillas (gluten-free)

Day 6:

Lunch: Baked potato topped with chili con carne (no onion)
Dinner: Grilled steak with roasted vegetables and herb-infused olive oil

Here is a cheap, simple, and flavour-packed 7-day meal plan designed specifically for your needs as a gluten-free, lactose-intolerant super-taster who dislikes extreme salt and sugar. ### Guiding Principles for Your Plan: * **Flavour First:** Focuses on fresh herbs, spices, garlic, ginger, and citrus to build deep flavour without relying on salt, sugar, or onion. * **Budget & Simplicity:** Uses affordable cuts of meat (chicken thighs, mince), versatile staples (potatoes, rice), and repeats ingredients to minimise waste. * **Gluten & Lactose Free:** All recipes are naturally free of these, with easy substitutions (e.g., tamari for soy sauce). * **Super-Taster Friendly:** Avoids overpowering saltiness and sweetness. You control the final seasoning. **Pro-Tip:** Cook a large batch of plain jasmine rice and plain roasted potatoes at the start of the week to use as bases for multiple meals. --- ### The 7-Day Meal Plan | Day | Lunch | Dinner | | :-- | :--- | :--- | | **Mon** | **Leftover Roast Chicken & Potato Bowl**
Shredded leftover chicken, roasted potatoes, and steamed green beans with a squeeze of lemon. | **Lemon & Herb Roast Chicken & Potatoes**
Roast chicken pieces and potato wedges with olive oil, garlic powder, fresh rosemary, and lemon juice. | | **Tue** | **Leftover Chicken & Rice**
The remaining shredded chicken served over jasmine rice with a side of sliced cucumber. | **"Deconstructed Taco" Bowl**
Seasoned beef mince (see seasoning mix below), jasmine rice, shredded lettuce, tomato, corn, and avocado. | | **Wed** | **Leftover Taco Bowl**
The leftover beef mince and rice reheated, served with fresh lettuce. | **Thai Basil Pork (Pad Krapow)**
Minced pork stir-fried with garlic, ginger, green beans, Thai basil, and a gluten-free sauce (see below). Served with jasmine rice and a fried egg. | | **Thu** | **Leftover Thai Basil Pork**
Reheated with a fresh squeeze of lime juice. | **Hearty Shepherd's Pie**
Gluten-free beef mince & pea filling topped with a creamy mash potato crust (use lactose-free milk and margarine). | | **Fri** | **Leftover Shepherd's Pie** | **"Fakeaway" Curry Night**
Use a store-bought **gluten-free & onion-free** Thai curry paste (e.g., Mae Ploy brand - check label). Simmer diced chicken and potatoes in coconut milk with the paste and bamboo shoots. Serve with rice. | | **Sat** | **Big Breakfast**
Scrambled eggs (with lactose-free milk), pan-fried bacon, and sautéed mushrooms with garlic. | **DIY Burger & Chips**
Beef patties on gluten-free buns with bacon, lettuce, tomato, and avocado. Oven-baked potato wedges ("chips") on the side. | | **Sun** | **Leftover Burger Patty & Salad**
Flake the leftover patty over a garden salad with a simple vinaigrette. | **Simple Roast Pork & Veg**
A cheap pork roast (loin or shoulder) rubbed with fennel seeds, garlic powder, and black pepper. Roast with carrots and potatoes. | --- ### Essential Flavour Builders & Recipes Your key to enjoying these meals is in the sauces and seasonings. Here’s how to make them without onion, gluten, or lactose. **1. Taco Seasoning (Salt-Free & Onion-Free)** * 2 tbsp ground cumin * 1 tbsp smoked paprika * 1 tbsp garlic powder * 2 tsp dried oregano * 1 tsp black pepper * (Optional) ½ tsp cayenne pepper for heat * Mix together. Use 2-3 tbsp per 500g of mince, adding a splash of water while cooking. **2. Gluten-Free Thai Stir-Fry Sauce (for Pad Krapow)** * 2 tbsp gluten-free fish sauce * 1 tbsp gluten-free soy sauce (tamari) * 1 tsp coconut sugar (or a tiny pinch of brown sugar) - omit if too sweet * 1-2 tbsp water * Mix and add to the pan at the end of cooking. **3. Simple Salad Vinaigrette** * 3 parts Extra Virgin Olive Oil * 1 part Acid (lemon juice, red wine vinegar, or apple cider vinegar) * A big pinch of dried herbs (oregano, thyme) or fresh herbs (parsley, basil) * Freshly cracked black pepper * Whisk vigorously. ### Shopping List **Produce:** * Garlic, Ginger, Fresh Lemons & Limes * Fresh Herbs: Rosemary, Thai Basil (or sweet basil), Parsley * Potatoes (a large bag) * Lettuce (iceberg or cos) * Tomatoes, Cucumber, Avocado * Green Beans, Carrots, Mushrooms * Corn (tinned or frozen is fine) **Protein:** * Chicken Thighs (1kg+) * Beef Mince (1kg+) * Pork Mince (500g) * Pork Roast (size of your choice) * Bacon * Eggs **Pantry:** * Jasmine Rice * Gluten-Free Soy Sauce (Tamari) * Gluten-Free Fish Sauce * Canned Coconut Milk * Gluten-Free & Onion-Free Thai Curry Paste (e.g., Mae Ploy Green or Red) * Spices: Cumin, Smoked Paprika, Garlic Powder, Oregano, Black Pepper, Fennel Seeds * Olive Oil * Lactose-Free Milk or Margarine (for mashing potatoes) This plan gives you variety within your favourite cuisines, keeps cooking simple, and ensures every meal is full of real flavour that won't overwhelm your palate. Enjoy
